content.problemStatement:
Write a program to reverse a linked list iteratively.


Input Format
The first input line will contain a single integer n, which denotes the number of nodes in the linked list.

The second line will contain n space-separated integers, where each integer represents the data of a node in the linked list.



Output Format
The output should be the reversed linked list printed as a space-separated sequence of node data.

code.Python3:
# Definition of Node class
class Node:
    def __init__(self, data):
        self.data = data
        self.next = None

def print_list(head):
    temp = head
    while temp:
        if temp.next:
            print(temp.data, end=" -> ")
        else:
            print(temp.data, end="")
        temp = temp.next
    print()

def reverse_iterative(head):
    prev = None
    current = head
    while current:
        next_node = current.next
        current.next = prev
        prev = current
        current = next_node
    return prev  # New head of the reversed list

def main():
    # Reading input
    n = int(input())  # Number of nodes
    if n == 0:
        print("")
        return
    
    # Reading the data for each node
    data_list = list(map(int, input().split()))  # This reads space-separated integers
    
    # Creating the linked list
    head = None
    temp = None
    for data in data_list:
        new_node = Node(data)
        if head is None:
            head = new_node
        else:
            temp.next = new_node
        temp = new_node

    # Reversing the list iteratively
    head = reverse_iterative(head)
    
    # Printing the reversed list
    print_list(head)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
